figures.caption: Figure 1. Architecture of the End-to-End Genomic Selection Decision Support119 System and the standardized six-step workflow. The left panel displays the Data Input Layer,120 supporting raw VCF files, phenotypic tables, and genotype matrices. The central core area121 illustrates the automated GUI pipeline comprising: (1) Quality Control (PLINK-based MAF and122 missing rate filtering), (2) Genotype Imputation, (3) Population Structure Analysis (PCA), (4)123 GWAS (Manhattan and QQ plots), (5) Genomic Prediction Engine (the core R+Python Dual-124 Engine architecture orchestrating various models), and (6) Result Integration & Export. The right125 panel represents the Output Layer, showcasing automatically generated comprehensive analytical126 charts and GEBV reports.127
Table 1. Summary of the 33 genomic prediction models integrated into the platform.145
Figure 2. Graphical User Interface (GUI) of the whole-genome selection platform featuring153

Figure 3. Evaluation of prediction accuracy (Pearson Correlation Coefficient) across six187 agronomic and quality traits using the Wheat2000 dataset. The bar chart illustrates the188 performance of 29 models (Four graph-topology-based models were excluded from this189 benchmark as the preceding PCA dimensionality reduction flattens the data, destroying the190 spatial topological structures required for their inputs.) distributed across four major algorithmic191 paradigms: Linear/Bayesian, Machine Learning (ML), Deep Learning (DL), and Hybrid192 architectures. The evaluated traits include thousand-kernel weight (TKW), test weight (TW),193 grain width (WIDTH), grain length (LENGTH), grain hardness (HARD), and protein content194 (PROT). Theoretical note on untested models: Four Graph Neural Network (GNN) models195 (GraphConvGS, GraphAttnGS, GraphSAGEGS, GraphFormer) integrated within the system196 were excluded from this benchmark. Theoretically, while traditional ML/DL models process197 flattened 2D matrices (0/1/2 SNP arrays or PCA-reduced features), GNNs operate on non-198 Euclidean data. They require inputs to be reconstructed into complex spatial topological graphs199 comprising nodes (samples) and edges (kinship/LD interactions). The standard PCA200 dimensionality reduction destroys the topological dependencies required for graph construction.201 Practically, computing these topological graphs relies on the torch_geometric library, which202 requires complex, hardware-specific C++/CUDA compilation. To preserve the absolute203 portability and zero-configuration compatibility of the system's dual-engine architecture on204 standard PCs, this heavy dependency was not bundled in the base environment (triggering a205 ModuleNotFoundError during automated batching). Consequently, these graph-topology-based206 models were excluded from this flat-matrix feature benchmark.207
